topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The delivery notification blinked insistently on my phone. A package. Addressed to Apartment 3B. My stomach clenched, a cold knot tightening beneath my ribs. I snatched the box from the hallway, the cardboard feeling flimsy and ominous in my hands. The handwriting on the label—it was definitely not mine. My breath hitched as I looked around my small living room, every shadow seeming to deepen, every creak of the floorboards sounding like a footstep behind me. Who would think this was mine? I dropped the package onto the worn rug, the sound startlingly loud in the silence. I backed away slowly, my eyes darting toward the front door, a frantic need to secure everything overriding all rational thought.

[story 2]

It arrived just as I was making my morning coffee. A plain brown box sat on the doorstep, a stark contrast to the usual mail. My heart began to hammer against my sternum, a frantic rhythm I couldn't seem to slow down. I approached it tentatively, my fingers trembling as I reached for the tape. The address clearly read 'Tenant 3B.' A wave of icy dread washed over me, making my palms instantly slick with sweat. I stared at the box, my jaw tight, unable to process the implication. I fumbled for my phone, my thumb hovering over the call button, my voice coming out thin and reedy when I finally managed to dial the building manager.

[story 3]

The doorbell rang, a sharp, insistent sound that ripped me from my focused reading. It was a package. My gaze dropped to the label, and a sudden, dizzying lurch seized my chest. It was clearly meant for the previous occupant. Every instinct screamed at me to run, to hide the object, to somehow erase the evidence of this intrusion. I stood frozen by the entryway, my hands gripping the doorframe so hard my knuckles were white. My breathing grew shallow, quick and uneven. I could feel the blood draining from my face, leaving a clammy residue, as I desperately searched for a way to make the package vanish before anyone else noticed.
